content.content:
450	];
451	
452	// ── OpCode stub ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
453	// TODO(port): OpCode should come from lua-code / lua_types when those land.
454	
455	/// Opcodes used directly by the parser. Subset of the full VM opcode set.
456	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
457	pub enum OpCode {
458	    Move,
459	    GetUpval,
460	    NewTable,
461	    SetList,
462	    Close,
463	    Tbc,          // OP_TBC
464	    Call,
465	    TailCall,
466	    Return,
467	    ForPrep,
468	    TForPrep,
469	    ForLoop,
470	    TForLoop,
471	    TForCall,
472	    Closure,
473	    Vararg,
474	    VarArgPrep,
475	}
476	
477	// ── Minimal LexState stub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
478	// PORT NOTE: In C, LexState is defined in llex.h (→ lua-lex crate).
479	//   We declare a minimal stub here for Phase A so function bodies can be
480	//   written. Phase B will replace with `lua_lex::LexState` and remove this.
481	
482	/// Semantic info attached to a token.
483	/// C: SemInfo from llex.h; Rust analogue is TokenValue.
484	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
485	pub struct TokenValue {
486	    /// C: r — float literal value (for TK_FLT)
487	    pub r: f64,
488	    /// C: i — integer literal value (for TK_INT)
489	    pub i: i64,
490	    /// C: ts — string value (for TK_NAME, TK_STRING)
491	    pub ts: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
492	}
493	
494	/// C: Token from llex.h.
495	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default)]
496	pub struct LexToken {
497	    pub token: TokenKind,
498	    pub seminfo: TokenValue,
499	}
500	
501	/// C: LexState — per-chunk lexer + parser state.
502	/// PORT NOTE: This is a Phase A stub. In Phase B, `LexState` lives in
503	///   `lua-lex` and `lua-parse` imports it. `FuncState` will move here
504	///   or be passed separately. The `fs` field creates a circular-crate
505	///   dependency that Phase B must resolve (likely: both live in one crate).
506	#[derive(Debug)]
507	pub struct LexState {
508	    /// C: current — current character (i32; -1 = EOZ)
509	    pub current: i32,
510	    /// C: linenumber
511	    pub linenumber: i32,
512	    /// C: lastline
513	    pub lastline: i32,
514	    /// C: t — current token
515	    pub t: LexToken,
516	    /// C: lookahead — one-token lookahead
517	    pub lookahead: LexToken,
518	    /// C: fs — current FuncState (parser owns this)
519	    pub fs: Option<Box<FuncState>>,
520	    /// C: dyd — parser dynamic data
521	    pub dyd: DynData,
522	    /// C: source — chunk name
523	    pub source: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
524	    /// C: envn — cached "_ENV" string
525	    pub envn: Option<GcRef<LuaString>>,
526	}
527	
528	// ── Minimal LuaState stub ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
529	// PORT NOTE: LuaState is defined in lua-vm. Referenced here as a placeholder.
530	//   Phase B will add `lua-vm` as a dep and remove this stub.
531	// TODO(port): remove LuaState stub when lua-vm dep lands.
532	pub struct LuaState;
533	
534	// ── Free functions ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
535	
536	// C: static void statement(LexState *ls);  -- forward declaration
537	// C: static void expr(LexState *ls, expdesc *v);  -- forward declaration
538	// (Both defined later in this file; Rust has no forward declarations.)
539	
540	// ── §1 Error helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
541	
542	/// C: static l_noret error_expected(LexState *ls, int token)
543	/// Constructs a syntax error for a missing expected token.
544	/// In Rust, `l_noret` becomes returning `LuaError`; callers use
545	/// `return Err(error_expected(...))`.
546	fn error_expected(ls: &LexState, token: TokenKind) -> LuaError {
547	    // C: luaX_syntaxerror(ls, luaO_pushfstring(ls->L, "%s expected", luaX_token2str(ls, token)));
548	    // TODO(port): lua_lex::token_to_str(ls, token) for the token name
549	    LuaError::syntax(format_args!("{} expected", token))

# type-vocabulary.tsv — canonical owners for cross-crate Rust types.
#
# Format:
#   <name> <kind> <owner-path> <mode> <notes>
#
# mode:
#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path
#   audit    audit reports it, but changed-file hook does not fail
#
# Keep this list small and intentional. It is not every Rust type in the
# workspace; it is the cross-cutting vocabulary whose duplication fragments the
# architecture.

LuaState        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical interpreter state
GlobalState     struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical shared VM state
CallInfo        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical call frame record
LuaValue        enum    crates/lua-types/src/value.rs    enforce  canonical TValue replacement
LuaError        enum    crates/lua-types/src/error.rs    enforce  canonical error type
LuaString       struct  crates/lua-types/src/string.rs   audit    currently duplicated in lua-vm; reconcile before enforce
LuaTable        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/table.rs       audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaProto        struct  crates/lua-types/src/proto.rs    audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
LuaClosure      enum    crates/lua-types/src/closure.rs  audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
UpVal           enum    crates/lua-types/src/upval.rs    audit    canonical upvalue shape
LuaUserData     struct  crates/lua-types/src/userdata.rs enforce  canonical userdata type
LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum
StackIdx        struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical stack index
CallInfoIdx     struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical call-info index
OpCode          enum    crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
Instruction     struct  crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce

# type-vocabulary.tsv — canonical owners for cross-crate Rust types.
#
# Format:
#   <name> <kind> <owner-path> <mode> <notes>
#
# mode:
#   enforce  hook fails if a modified file defines this name outside owner-path
#   audit    audit reports it, but changed-file hook does not fail
#
# Keep this list small and intentional. It is not every Rust type in the
# workspace; it is the cross-cutting vocabulary whose duplication fragments the
# architecture.

LuaState        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical interpreter state
GlobalState     struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical shared VM state
CallInfo        struct  crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs       enforce  canonical call frame record
LuaValue        enum    crates/lua-types/src/value.rs    enforce  canonical TValue replacement
LuaError        enum    crates/lua-types/src/error.rs    enforce  canonical error type
LuaString       struct  crates/lua-types/src/string.rs   audit    currently duplicated in lua-vm; reconcile before enforce

LuaClosure      enum    crates/lua-types/src/closure.rs  audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
UpVal           enum    crates/lua-types/src/upval.rs    audit    canonical upvalue shape
LuaUserData     struct  crates/lua-types/src/userdata.rs enforce  canonical userdata type
LuaStatus       enum    crates/lua-types/src/status.rs   enforce  canonical status enum
StackIdx        struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical stack index
CallInfoIdx     struct  crates/lua-types/src/lib.rs      enforce  canonical call-info index
OpCode          enum    crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce
Instruction     struct  crates/lua-code/src/opcodes.rs   audit    currently duplicated/placeheld; reconcile before enforce

content.text:
Done. Reporting:

## Reconcile-types report: lua-parse

**Violations (before → after):**
- FAIL: 1 → 0 (`struct LuaState` duplicate eliminated)
- WARN: 1 → 0 (`enum OpCode` duplicate eliminated; was audit-only, cleaned up as a bonus since the file already touched cargo deps)

**Cargo deps added to `crates/lua-parse/Cargo.toml`:**
- `lua-vm.workspace = true` (canonical owner of `LuaState`)
- `lua-code.workspace = true` (canonical owner of `OpCode`)

**Code changes (`crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs`):**
- Replaced the `pub struct LuaState;` stub with `pub use lua_vm::state::LuaState;`
- Replaced the local `pub enum OpCode { … }` (17 variants) with `pub use lua_code::opcodes::OpCode;` — the canonical enum is a superset and contains every variant lua-parse used (`Move`, `GetUpval/GetUpVal`, `NewTable`, `SetList`, `Close`, `Tbc`, `Call`, `TailCall`, `Return`, `ForPrep`, `TForPrep`, `ForLoop`, `TForLoop`, `TForCall`, `Closure`, `Vararg`, `VarArgPrep`).

**`todo!()` stubs introduced:** 0. The canonical `LuaState` already exposes the surface lua-parse references (or those refs were already in `// TODO(port):` comments), so no method/field gaps surfaced. `cargo check -p lua-parse` is clean (0 errors, warnings only — all pre-existing).

**Unresolved:** None within scope. Workspace-level errors in `lua-cli`, `lua-lex`, `lua-gc` are pre-existing and out of scope (touching them would violate the "edit only `crates/lua-parse/`" constraint).

## Reconcile-types report: lua-parse

**Violations (before → after):**
- FAIL: 1 → 0 (`struct LuaState` duplicate eliminated)
- WARN: 1 → 0 (`enum OpCode` duplicate eliminated; was audit-only, cleaned up as a bonus since the file already touched cargo deps)

**Cargo deps added to `crates/lua-parse/Cargo.toml`:**
- `lua-vm.workspace = true` (canonical owner of `LuaState`)
- `lua-code.workspace = true` (canonical owner of `OpCode`)

**Code changes (`crates/lua-parse/src/lib.rs`):**
- Replaced the `pub struct LuaState;` stub with `pub use lua_vm::state::LuaState;`
- Replaced the local `pub enum OpCode { … }` (17 variants) with `pub use lua_code::opcodes::OpCode;` — the canonical enum is a superset and contains every variant lua-parse used (`Move`, `GetUpval/GetUpVal`, `NewTable`, `SetList`, `Close`, `Tbc`, `Call`, `TailCall`, `Return`, `ForPrep`, `TForPrep`, `ForLoop`, `TForLoop`, `TForCall`, `Closure`, `Vararg`, `VarArgPrep`).

**`todo!()` stubs introduced:** 0. The canonical `LuaState` already exposes the surface lua-parse references (or those refs were already in `// TODO(port):` comments), so no method/field gaps surfaced. `cargo check -p lua-parse` is clean (0 errors, warnings only — all pre-existing).

**Unresolved:** None within scope. Workspace-level errors in `lua-cli`, `lua-lex`, `lua-gc` are pre-existing and out of scope (touching them would violate the "edit only `crates/lua-parse/`" constraint).
